intro: Migrating from TypeScript to Dart shifts developers from a structural, erasure-based type system built on top of JavaScript to a nominal, sound type system engineered for native compilation. Dart eliminates runtime surprises caused by JavaScript's dynamic underpinnings through strict null safety and ahead-of-time compilation. Teams typically make this transition when moving from web-centric ecosystems to cross-platform mobile development with Flutter.

diffs.from: Structural typing where shapes match regardless of declarations, and types are completely erased at runtime.
diffs.to: Nominal typing where class lineage matters, backed by sound null safety enforced at both compile time and runtime.

diffs.from: Uses JavaScript-derived syntax with keywords like 'let', 'const', interfaces, and flexible object literals.
diffs.to: Uses C-style class-based syntax with 'var', 'final', 'const', and replaces interfaces with implicit class interfaces or mixins.

diffs.from: Managed by the host JavaScript engine's garbage collector, which is optimized for web page lifecycles.
diffs.to: Managed by Dart's generational garbage collector, specifically optimized for high-velocity UI allocation and rendering.

diffs.from: Event loop model utilizing Promises, async/await keywords, and RxJS for stream management.
diffs.to: Single-threaded event loop utilizing Futures and native, built-in Stream classes with async* generators.

diffs.from: Minimal standard library heavily reliant on the npm ecosystem for basic utilities, collections, and math.
diffs.to: Comprehensive, battery-included core library (dart:core, dart:collection, dart:async, dart:convert) built into the SDK.

examples.title: Data Transfer Object with Methods
examples.description: Converting a structural TypeScript interface and class implementation to a nominal Dart class with an implicit interface.

examples.title: Asynchronous Data Fetching
examples.description: Porting an async fetch operation handling JSON deserialization.

tips.title: Ditch Structural Literals
tips.body: Stop declaring inline anonymous object shapes; use strongly typed Dart classes or explicit Map<K, V> types instead.

tips.title: Understand Implicit Interfaces
tips.body: Dart does not have an 'interface' keyword. Every class implicitly defines an interface containing all its instance fields and methods, allowing any class to be implemented.

tips.title: Leverage Factory Constructors
tips.body: Use Dart's 'factory' keyword to implement constructors that do not always create new instances, ideal for caching or initializing subclasses.

tips.title: Enforce Strict Null Safety
tips.body: Dart types are non-nullable by default. Append a question mark (e.g., String?) only when a value can explicitly be null, and avoid using the '!' bang operator blindly.
